clinical_significance: High risk of serious bacterial infection (SBI)
May be life-threatening even if baby appears well

history: Birth history (PROM, maternal fever, NICU stay)

parent_counseling: Neonatal fever is serious and requires admission — नवजात शिशु में बुखार गंभीर है और भर्ती होना ज़रूरी है।
Early treatment improves outcome — जल्दी इलाज शुरू करने से बच्चा जल्दी ठीक होता है।
Avoid overheating or over-wrapping — बच्चे को ज़्यादा कपड़े या गर्मी में न रखें।
Watch for danger signs — खतरे के संकेतों पर ध्यान रखें।
warning_signs: Poor feeding or refusal to feed
expected_course: Depends on underlying cause. Bacterial infections require 7-14 days of antibiotics. Viral infections may resolve in 3-5 days. HSV requires 14-21 days of acyclovir. Early sepsis workup and empirical antibiotics are critical as delays worsen prognosis significantly.
key_clinical_points: All febrile neonates (<28 days) must be admitted for full sepsis workup.
Send all cultures BEFORE starting antibiotics.
HSV must be considered in any febrile neonate, especially with vesicular rash, seizures, or liver involvement.
Even a well-appearing febrile neonate has significant risk of serious bacterial infection.
Hypothermia in a neonate is as concerning as fever.

severity_assessment.mild: Low-grade fever with well-appearing neonate, normal feeding, and no risk factors -- still requires full workup and admission.
severity_assessment.moderate: Fever with reduced feeding, mild irritability, or maternal risk factors (PROM, GBS).
severity_assessment.severe: High fever with lethargy, seizures, respiratory distress, poor perfusion, bulging fontanelle, or hemodynamic instability.
